stem: Find the range of the data set $5, 12, 3, 9, 7$.
explanation: The highest value is $12$ and the lowest is $3$. Range $= 12 - 3 = 9$.

stem: Find the range of the data set $20, 6, 14, 2, 18$.
explanation: The highest value is $20$ and the lowest is $2$. Range $= 20 - 2 = 18$.

stem: Find the range of the data set $-3, 4, 11, 0, 6$.
hint: Range = highest value − lowest value. Take care with negative values.
explanation: The highest value is $11$ and the lowest is $-3$. Range $= 11 - (-3) = 11 + 3 = 14$.

stem: The scores in a quiz are $8, 15, 3, 22, 11$. Find the range of the scores.
explanation: The highest score is $22$ and the lowest is $3$. Range $= 22 - 3 = 19$.

stem: Find the interquartile range (IQR) of the data set $2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12$.
hint: Split the data into a lower half and an upper half. IQR = median of upper half − median of lower half.

stem: Find the interquartile range (IQR) of the data set $1, 5, 9, 13, 17, 21$.
hint: Split into lower half $\{1, 5, 9\}$ and upper half $\{13, 17, 21\}$, then find each median.

stem: Find the interquartile range (IQR) of the data set $3, 6, 8, 14, 16, 20$.
hint: Split into lower half $\{3, 6, 8\}$ and upper half $\{14, 16, 20\}$, then find each median.

stem: Find the interquartile range (IQR) of the data set $10, 14, 18, 24, 30, 36$.
hint: Split into lower half $\{10, 14, 18\}$ and upper half $\{24, 30, 36\}$, then find each median.

stem:
Which data set has the greater range?

Set A: $\{8, 9, 10, 11, 12\}$

Set B: $\{3, 7, 10, 13, 17\}$
options: Both sets have the same range
hint: Calculate the range (max − min) for each set, then compare.
explanation: Set A: range $= 12 - 8 = 4$. Set B: range $= 17 - 3 = 14$. Set B has the greater range.

stem:
Which data set is more consistent?

Set A: $\{15, 17, 19, 21, 23\}$

Set B: $\{5, 12, 19, 26, 33\}$
hint: A more consistent data set has less spread. Compare the ranges.
explanation: Set A: range $= 23 - 15 = 8$. Set B: range $= 33 - 5 = 28$. Set A has the smaller range, so it is more consistent.

stem:
Which data set has the greater IQR?

Set A: $\{4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14\}$

Set B: $\{2, 5, 8, 11, 14, 17\}$
options: Both sets have the same IQR
hint: Find Q1 and Q3 for each set, then calculate IQR = Q3 − Q1.
explanation: Set A: Q1 $= 6$, Q3 $= 12$, IQR $= 6$. Set B: Q1 $= 5$, Q3 $= 14$, IQR $= 9$. Set B has the greater IQR.

stem:
Two data sets are summarised below.

Set A: range $= 12$, IQR $= 4$

Set B: range $= 10$, IQR $= 8$

Which statement best describes the comparison?
options: Set A has greater spread by both measures
Set B has greater spread by both measures
Set A has greater overall spread, but Set B has greater spread in the middle 50% of values
Set B has greater overall spread, but Set A has greater spread in the middle 50% of values
answer: Set A has greater overall spread, but Set B has greater spread in the middle 50% of values
hint: The range measures overall spread. The IQR measures spread in the middle 50% of values.
explanation: Set A has a greater range ($12 > 10$), so it has greater overall spread. Set B has a greater IQR ($8 > 4$), so it has greater spread in the middle 50% of values.
